mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equities declines and risk appetite improves, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the rate cut is part of an active easing cycle rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines on a 3-month trend basis but SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of such periods or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp inflation slowdown in CPIAUCSL implies lower expected future policy tightening and lower real-rate pressure on equities, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPI disinflation episodes followed by stable or rising FEDFUNDS still produce negative SPX 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the interaction effect is not positive in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks labor-market deterioration, which weakens earnings expectations and credit sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if labor-deterioration triggers are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 40% of observations or if the signal’s hit rate falls below baseline by any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a broad payroll recession, which should depress equity earnings expectations and push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition does not outperform either series alone and does not produce negative SPX 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real activity reflected by declining GDPC1 and weak RSAFS signals falling aggregate demand, which should reduce forward earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative and RSAFS 3-month growth is below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDP-sales growth combinations fail to precede negative SPX 60-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction coefficient is not significantly below zero in rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial supply conditions and stronger cyclical output, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month through better earnings momentum and risk-on sentiment.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is followed by negative SPX 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances or if the signal loses predictive power after 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior slump signals renewed housing demand and easier financial conditions, which should lift broad equity returns over the next 20 trading days as cyclicals and household wealth expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change turns positive after a 6-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebounds during unchanged or restrictive policy do not lead to positive SPX 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with sticky CPIAUCSL keeps real rates elevated and compresses equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate periods with persistent inflation do not precede negative SPX 60-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if returns are positive despite sustained restrictive policy and sticky CPI.
